title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
description: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.

title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
description: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

title: Sandstone raises $30M to bring AI to in-house legal teams
description: Sandstone's Series A comes just six months after a Sequoia-led seed round.

title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
description: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
description: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
description: Can Apple's new AI glow-up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
description: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
description: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
description: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
description: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
description: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
description: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

title: Apple just taught your iPhone to finish your sentences, your photos, and your workflows
description: Apple is adding new AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps.

title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
description: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade, letting you describe the workflow you want in a prompt.
